cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The problem says today is the second day of the third month of 1966. So that's March 2, 1966. I need to find out what the date was a month ago. So, going back one month from March 2, 1966. First, going back one month from March would take us to February. So the month would be February 1966. But what day? If today is March 2, then a month ago would be February 2, 1966? Wait, but months have different numbers of days. For example, February 1966 had 28 days because 1966 is not a leap year. Leap years are divisible by 4, but 1966 divided by 4 is 491.5, so not a leap year. So February 1966 had 28 days. But when you subtract a month from a date, you don't necessarily just subtract 30 days. For example, if today is March 2, and I go back one month, that's February 2. Because if you go back 30 days, March 2 minus 30 days would be February 2, but if you go back exactly one month, then it's just the same day in the previous month. So for example, March 1 minus one month is February 1, March 2 minus one month is February 2, etc., unless the previous month doesn't have that day. For example, if it was March 31, and you go back one month, there is no February 31, so you'd go to February 28 or 29. In this case, since February 2 exists, then March 2 minus one month is February 2. So the answer would be 02/02/1966, which is option C. Let me check the options again. The options are: A) 02/01/1966 B) 12/17/1965 C) 02/02/1966 D) 03/18/1966 E) 02/03/1966 So option C is 02/02/1966. That seems correct. Wait, but let me double-check. Let's think of another approach. Let's count the number of days between February 2 and March 2. February 2 to February 28 is 27 days (since we start counting from February 2 as day 0). Then March 1 and March 2 add 2 more days.
